concerns.text: Success criterion SC-006 states 'of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - the percentage value is missing (appears as blank). This makes the criterion untestable. Same issue in SC-012. These must have explicit percentage thresholds for scientific validity.

At crossing number 13, there are only 49 prime knots (per OEIS A002863). For regression and correlation analysis, this sample size may lack statistical power. The plan acknowledges ≤10 validation scope but should explicitly state that crossing number 11-13 analysis is exploratory due to limited sample size.

Braid index computation for higher crossing numbers may have uncertainty - Knot Atlas may not have verified braid index values for all knots. The plan should explicitly document braid index uncertainty thresholds and distinguish between tabulated vs. algorithmically computed values.
